title: Schoenus foliatus
taxonomy: Schoenus foliatus is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This family is a large group of monocotyledonous flowering plants commonly known as sedges, rushes, and cattails. The genus Schoenus is a group of sedges that are typically found in wetland environments. Schoenus foliatus is native to Australia and is often found in freshwater wetlands, particularly in the tropical and subtropical regions. It is a rhizomatous perennial herb with narrow, strap-like leaves and a cylindrical inflorescence.

intro: Schoenus foliatus, also known as the yellow sedge, is a perennial plant belonging to the Cyperaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Schoenus foliatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
